headline: Salesforce Sees Tepid Demand for Bond Sale to Fund Share Buyback
summary: The company, which makes customer relationship management software, drew $50 billion of demand at its peak, according to people with knowledge of the matter.  The software firm is raising funds from an eight-part bond offering, with maturities ranging from two to 40 years, the people said, asking not to be identified because details are private.

raw.headline: Salesforce Sees Tepid Demand for Bond Sale to Fund Share Buyback

headline: Salesforce's $25B bond sale signals it's 'content' without M&A
summary: Salesforce (CRM) has its sights set on raising $25 billion through a new US bond offering as means to fund stock buybacks, according to Bloomberg. RBC Capital Markets managing director Rishi Jaluria reacts to this headline and what it illustrates about the company. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: Salesforce Headlines Another Big Day for Corporate Borrowing
summary: Salesforce is working on an eight-part bond sale Wednesday to fund share buybacks, hoping to capitalize on improving investor attitudes toward the software sector.  A day after Amazon.com headlined a record $66 billion of U.S. investment-grade bond sales, Salesforce is set to issue as much as $25 billion in new bonds, according to an investor familiar with the matter.  Overall, companies are expected to issue around $35 billion to $40 billion of bonds, another huge day after bond issuance had previously slowed in response to market volatility caused by the Iran war.

raw.headline: Salesforce Headlines Another Big Day for Corporate Borrowing

Overall, companies are expected to issue around $35 billion to $40 billion of bonds, another huge day after bond issuance had previously slowed in response to market volatility caused by the Iran war.

headline: K1 Invests in Spin.AI, a Leader in AI-Powered Cloud Security and Data Protection
summary: K1 Investment Management, LLC ("K1"), one of the largest investors in small-cap AI-powered software, today officially announced its investment in Spin.AI, a security and data protection platform purpose-built to protect the cloud environments where modern enterprises run some of their most critical operations.

headline: Credibly Streamlines Merchant Application Process, Expands Broker Integration with Cloudsquare
summary: Credibly, the working capital company that champions small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs), announced today the launch of a streamlined Online Checkout experience and the release of Credibly API V2 alongside a new integration with Cloudsquare Broker, a secure Salesforce-powered CRM built for alternative lenders.
